scheduled_followups.what: task #169 closed early on user override 2026-04-28 22:51Z; substrate watch through window-end. scheduled_followups.why: On 2026-05-01 bg_loop_silent (sev2) fired immediately on first deploy with loop=health_monitor, age_s=250. Investigation showed no real outage — the bug was a calibration drift: bg_heartbeat.EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S["health_monitor"]=60 vs actual asyncio.sleep(300) in health_monitor.py:101. Fix landed e820ca85. The same drift class will bite again as new loops are added or sleep cadences change. This task spawns an audit fork to catch all such drifts + recommend an AST-grep CI gate.
scheduled_followups.doc: CLAUDE.md Session 214 entry on bg_heartbeat calibration; commit e820ca85
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn a research fork: walk EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S in mcp-server/central-command/backend/bg_heartbeat.py + task_defs in mcp-server/main.py; for each entry, find the corresponding loop function and grep its inter-iteration asyncio.sleep(N); flag any drift > 50%. Also list loops in task_defs without EXPECTED_INTERVAL_S coverage. Recommend an AST-grep CI gate. Report under 600 words. Research-only — no code changes from the fork.

scheduled_followups.why: CronCreate scheduled cron e6242204 for Monday 8:43 AM local but the durable flag appears non-functional in this runtime build (response said Session-only despite durable=true). Without the cross-session pattern this entry, the digest stops the moment my session ends. Future-Claude on first invocation should: (1) run the digest fork itself, (2) re-schedule the cron with CronCreate("43 8 * * 1", recurring=true, durable=true), (3) bump THIS scheduled_followups entry to next Monday to keep the self-healing chain alive.
scheduled_followups.doc: CLAUDE.md Session 214 entry on bg_heartbeat calibration (sibling pattern); CronCreate id e6242204 (in-session) — may have died
scheduled_followups.action_prompt: Spawn a research fork: read .agent/claude-progress.json::scheduled_followups, bucket entries by days_until_due (OVERDUE / DUE_THIS_WEEK / UPCOMING / LATER), cross-check any named substrate invariants against prod via SSH. Emit a markdown digest under 800 words. AFTER digest: re-schedule the weekly cron via CronCreate AND bump this scheduled_followups entry due-date to next Monday. Self-healing chain.
